record.field: Fonoster is a self-hosted, programmable voice platform that brings Twilio-style telephony APIs to your own infrastructure.

Fonoster is a self-hosted, programmable voice platform that brings Twilio-style telephony APIs to your own infrastructure.

Built on Asterisk and its own cloud-first SIP server (Routr), Fonoster lets developers build and run voice applications as small Node.js apps that react to call events such as answer, hangup and DTMF. It exposes an API-first stack covering SIP signaling, media handling, PSTN connectivity and speech, all deployable with Docker or Kubernetes. It targets developers, integrators and businesses that want full control over their telephony without a proprietary cloud provider.

Key features
  • Programmable voice apps with verbs like Play, Say, Gather and Hangup for IVRs and call flows
  • SIP network with Domains, Agents, Trunks and ACLs for softphones, IP phones and PSTN connectivity
  • Text-to-Speech and Automatic Speech Recognition for voice-driven interactions
  • SDKs for Node.js and the browser (NPM/CDN) plus a CLI to build and deploy quickly
  • Call recording with S3-compatible storage for sounds and recordings
  • OAuth2/JWT authentication and Docker/Kubernetes-ready deployment
Best for

Developers and businesses building IVRs, voice assistants, contact-center workflows or PBX/SIP trunking on their own infrastructure, who want a programmable, open-source alternative to Twilio.

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
# Placeholder - RSA keys need to be generated manually after deployment

# Envoy configuration without tls for development and testing. # Do not use this configuration in production. Please check the docs for examples using tls. static_resources: listeners: - name: listener_http address: socket_address: { address: 0.0.0.0, port_value: 8449 } filter_chains: - filters: - name: envoy.filters.network.http_connection_manager typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager codec_type: auto stat_prefix: ingress_http route_config: name: local_route virtual_hosts: - name: local_service domains: ["*"] routes: - match: prefix: "/api" route: cluster: apiserver-cluster-http timeout: 0s - match: prefix: "/" headers: - name: "content-type" safe_regex_match: google_re2: {} regex: "^(application/grpc|application/grpc-web-text)$" route: cluster: apiserver-cluster timeout: 0s max_stream_duration: grpc_timeout_header_max: 0s - match: prefix: "/" route: cluster: dashboard-cluster timeout: 0s cors: allow_origin_string_match: - prefix: "*" allow_methods: GET, PUT, DELETE, POST, OPTIONS allow_headers: token,accesskeyid,keep-alive,user-agent,cache-control,content-type,content-transfer-encoding,x-accept-content-transfer-encoding,x-accept-response-streaming,x-user-agent,x-grpc-web,grpc-timeout max_age: "1728000" expose_headers: grpc-status,grpc-message http_filters: - name: envoy.filters.http.grpc_web typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.grpc_web.v3.GrpcWeb - name: envoy.filters.http.cors typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.cors.v3.Cors - name: envoy.filters.http.router typed_config: "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.router.v3.Router
clusters: - name: apiserver-cluster type: logical_dns connect_timeout: 20s http2_protocol_options: {} lb_policy: round_robin load_assignment: cluster_name: apiserver-cluster endpoints: - lb_endpoints: - endpoint: address: socket_address: address: apiserver port_value: 50051
- name: apiserver-cluster-http type: logical_dns connect_timeout: 20s lb_policy: round_robin load_assignment: cluster_name: apiserver-cluster-http endpoints: - lb_endpoints: - endpoint: address: socket_address: address: apiserver port_value: 9876
- name: dashboard-cluster type: logical_dns connect_timeout: 20s lb_policy: round_robin load_assignment: cluster_name: dashboard-cluster endpoints: - lb_endpoints: - endpoint: address: socket_address: address: dashboard port_value: 3030
